description: A direct model call answering an arithmetic question from weights alone — no tools, one call, no verification. Calibrated to MEASURED behavior (Experiment 007, 2026-07-15): Claude answers 127 × 49 correctly, but the same architecture is confidently wrong on larger products. The teaching point is unverifiability, not constant error. Used by the flagship workflows-vs-agents lesson.

turns.teaching: No tools were offered and none exist. The model answered CORRECTLY here — but nothing verified it, and you cannot tell a right answer from a wrong one by looking. On larger products the same architecture is confidently, consistently wrong (Experiment 007). Correctness you cannot verify is not correctness you can depend on.
